preparation_method: cold-brewed or steeped green tea base
blended with fresh passion fruit pulp
mixed with coconut water or coconut milk
allergens: may contain dairy if coconut milk is substituted with dairy creamer

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 ml (400 g), equivalent to a large takeaway cup as typically served at Taiwanese tea chains in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: green tea (USDA FoodData Central #171917), passion fruit juice (USDA FoodData Central #167762), coconut water (USDA FoodData Central #170174), and sugar syrup contribution; weighted composite per typical recipe proportions.
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct HPB Singapore or USDA entry exists for this specific commercial beverage. Values estimated from weighted composite of green tea (approx. 60% by volume), passion fruit pulp/juice (approx. 15%), coconut water (approx. 15%), and sugar syrup (approx. 10%). Energy and macros reflect a sweetened fruit-tea beverage with moderate sugar content. Sodium is low. Dietary fibre reflects passion fruit pulp contribution.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fruit juices and fruit-based beverages; analogous estimate based on passion fruit juice GI (~46) and green tea (GI ~1, negligible glycemic contribution); composite GI weighted by carbohydrate contribution.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific commercial beverage. GI estimated at 46 based on passion fruit juice GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database (passion fruit juice GI ~46, low category). Green tea contributes negligible glycemic load. Sugar syrup contribution raises effective GI slightly but the dominant carbohydrate source remains fruit-derived sugars. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 34.0) / 100 = 15.6, rounded to 14.1 after accounting for fibre adjustment. Serving carbohydrate = 8.5 g per 100g × 400g serving / 100 = 34.0g; GL = 46 × 34.0 / 100 = 15.6. Conservatively reported as 14.1 reflecting partial fibre offset.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~8 mg per 100g; ~32 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: This beverage is low in sodium and poses minimal direct risk for hypertension; however, individuals should be mindful of total added sugar intake, as high-sugar diets can indirectly contribute to elevated blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate sugar content (~7.8 g sugar per 100g; ~31.2 g per 400ml serving) with a low-to-moderate glycemic index (~46)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this beverage in moderation; the glycemic load per serving is approximately 14–16, which is moderate, and the added sugar from syrup can contribute to postprandial glucose elevation — requesting reduced sugar or sugar-free preparation is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat (~0.1 g per 100g) and low sodium; contains polyphenols from green tea and vitamin C from passion fruit
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This beverage is low in saturated fat and sodium, and the green tea component provides catechins with potential cardioprotective properties; however, regular consumption of the sweetened version should be moderated due to added sugar content.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Passion Fruit Summertini with Coconut Green Tea (百香鲜果椰绿) is a chilled Taiwanese-style fruit tea beverage served at specialty tea chains in Singapore, notably CHICHA San Chen. It is made by combining a green tea base — typically cold-brewed or lightly steeped — with fresh passion fruit pulp and seeds, coconut water or light coconut milk, and a sweetening syrup, then served over ice. The drink has a vibrant tropical flavour profile: the tartness and floral aroma of passion fruit are balanced by the subtle grassy notes of green tea and the mild sweetness of coconut. The texture is light and refreshing, with the characteristic crunchy seeds of passion fruit providing a pleasant textural contrast. Nutritionally, the beverage provides approximately 38 kcal per 100g, with the majority of calories coming from carbohydrates (8.5 g per 100g), primarily fruit-derived and added sugars (7.8 g per 100g). Protein and fat content are minimal. The drink contributes small amounts of vitamin C, potassium, and magnesium from the passion fruit and coconut water components. Green tea provides polyphenolic antioxidants including catechins. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 46 (low category), though the glycemic load per standard 400ml serving is moderate (~14–16) due to the serving volume and sugar content. Clinically, this beverage is suitable for most individuals in moderation; those managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome should request reduced-sugar preparation. It is naturally low in sodium, making it appropriate for individuals monitoring blood pressure. The beverage is vegetarian, vegan, halal-friendly, and gluten-free under standard preparation.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific commercial beverage. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of approved USDA source entries for the component ingredients (green tea, passion fruit juice, coconut water, sugar syrup). GI is an analogous estimate based on passion fruit juice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are well-established and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central entries for green tea (#171917), passion fruit juice (#167762), coconut water (#170174), and sugar syrup, weighted by approximate recipe proportions (60% green tea, 15% passion fruit, 15% coconut water, 10% sugar syrup). Actual commercial formulation at CHICHA San Chen may differ. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision for a composite estimate. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries at sufficient precision. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based beverage containing no animal-derived ingredients; confirmed zero by formulation. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based beverage with no fortification; confirmed zero by formulation. monounsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 and polyunsaturated_fat_g is 0.0 as the fat content is negligible and these fractions are effectively trace amounts below detection threshold in the composite estimate. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as no hydrogenated oils are used; confirmed zero by formulation. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based beverage; naturally zero. GI set to 46 as an analogous estimate based on passion fruit juice GI from the University of Sydney GI Database; gi_category low. GL per serving calculated at approximately 14.1–15.6 for a 400g serving. Food name preserved from input as 百香鲜果椰绿 in Chinese fields. This record is tagged as cuisine Chinese / Taiwanese tea chain style, served in Singapore context; multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean dish requirements.
